responses: Hello! Welcome to student support. How can I help you today?

patterns: what are you able to help with
responses: I can help with admissions, fees, exams, results, hostels, transport, library hours and more. What do you need?
Sure, ask me about fees, course units, exam timetables, or student services.
I answer questions on school hours, admissions, fees, results and general student support.
Go ahead and ask your question — I'll do my best to answer.
I'm your student support assistant. Try asking about fees, exams, or hostel info.

patterns: what time does the school close
when can i visit the office
responses: Our offices are open Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
We operate from 8 AM to 5 PM on weekdays.
School offices open at 8:00 AM and close at 5:00 PM, Monday through Friday.
Weekday hours are 8 AM to 5 PM.
You can visit us Monday to Friday between 8:00 AM and 5:00 PM.

patterns: is the office open on weekends
are you open on weekends too
responses: On Saturdays we're open from 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM. We're closed on Sundays.
Saturday hours are 9 AM to 1 PM; we remain closed on Sundays.
Yes, half-day on Saturday: 9 AM to 1 PM. Closed Sundays.
We open briefly on Saturday mornings (9 AM to 1 PM) and stay closed on Sunday.
Saturday: 9 AM–1 PM. Sunday: closed.

patterns: how much do i pay per semester
how much does the course cost
what is the fee for this term
responses: Fees vary by course. Please check the official fee structure on the notice board or bursar's office.
You can get the exact fee structure for your programme from the finance office.
Fee amounts depend on your course and year of study — the bursar's office has the current structure.
The finance office publishes the fee structure each semester; I'd recommend checking there for exact figures.
Tuition varies per programme. Visit the accounts office for the current breakdown.

patterns: what payment methods do you accept
can i pay fees via mpesa
how do i pay school fees
is bank transfer accepted for fees
responses: You can pay via M-Pesa Paybill, bank deposit, or directly at the accounts office.
We accept M-Pesa, bank transfer, and cash payments at the finance office.
Fees can be paid through M-Pesa Paybill or bank deposit — the paybill number is on your admission letter.
Payment options include M-Pesa, direct bank deposit, and installment plans approved by the bursar.
You may pay via M-Pesa or bank transfer; installment arrangements can be discussed with the accounts office.

patterns: how do i join the institute
what qualifications do i need to enroll
what documents do i need for admission
responses: You need a KCSE certificate with the minimum mean grade required for your chosen course, plus a copy of your ID and passport photos.
Admission requires your KCSE results slip/certificate, national ID copy, and passport-size photos submitted to the admissions office.
Minimum entry grades depend on the course — check with the admissions office for your specific programme.
Bring your KCSE certificate, ID, birth certificate and passport photos to the admissions office to begin the process.
Requirements typically include a KCSE certificate, ID copy and passport photos — the admissions office can confirm course-specific grades.

patterns: do you offer a diploma in ai
which certificate courses do you have
responses: We offer diplomas and certificates in ICT, Artificial Intelligence, Business, Electrical Engineering and more.
Available programmes include IT-related diplomas, engineering courses, and business certificates — check the prospectus for the full list.
Yes, we offer an AI Diploma along with several other ICT and engineering programmes.
Our course catalogue covers ICT, AI, electrical and business programmes at certificate and diploma level.
You can find the full list of courses in the institute's prospectus or on the admissions notice board.

patterns: when is the end of semester exam
when will i sit my exams
when does the exam period begin
responses: Exam timetables are released by the exams office two weeks before the exam period.
Check the notice board or student portal for the official exam timetable once released.
The exams office publishes the schedule near the end of each semester — keep checking the portal.
Exam dates vary per semester; the exams office will post the timetable in advance.
You'll find the exact dates on the exam timetable posted by the exams office.

patterns: how do i check my results
where can i see my exam results
how do i access my grades
how do i know if i passed
responses: Results are usually posted on the student portal about three weeks after exams.
You can check your results by logging into the student portal with your admission number.
Results release dates are announced by the exams office; check the portal or notice board.
Log in to the student portal to view your grades once they're released.
Results typically come out a few weeks after exams — the portal will notify you.

patterns: how do i register for units
how do i select my units
how to register for this semester
can i add or drop a unit
responses: Unit registration is done online through the student portal at the start of each semester.
Log into the student portal, select your units for the semester, and submit before the deadline.
Registration opens at the start of the semester; deadlines are posted on the notice board.
You can add or drop units within the first two weeks of the semester through the portal.
Visit the academic office if you have trouble registering units on the portal.

patterns: what time does the library open
is the library open on weekends
can i study in the library at night
responses: The library is open Monday to Friday, 8 AM to 8 PM, and Saturday 9 AM to 1 PM.
Library hours are 8 AM–8 PM on weekdays and a half-day on Saturday.
During exam periods the library extends its hours — check the library notice board for details.
You can borrow books any weekday between 8 AM and 8 PM.
The library opens at 8 AM and closes at 8 PM, Monday to Friday.

patterns: how do i book a hostel room
responses: Yes, hostel accommodation is available; apply through the student affairs office once admitted.
Hostel fees are charged per semester and vary by room type — check with student affairs for rates.
Rooms are usually shared by 2–4 students; book early as spaces are limited.
You can apply for a hostel room through the student affairs office after confirming admission.
Accommodation is available but limited, so early application is recommended.

patterns: how do i get to campus
responses: The institute does not run a dedicated bus, but several matatus pass near the main gate.
Public transport (matatus) serves the area around campus regularly.
There's no official school bus, but the campus is well served by local matatu routes.
Check with student affairs about any partner transport services for students.
Matatus running the main route stop close to the institute gate throughout the day.

patterns: how do i reset my portal password
i can't log into the portal
i forgot my student id password
who do i contact for tech issues
responses: Visit the ICT department or email the helpdesk to reset your portal password.
For login issues, the ICT support office can reset your credentials — bring your student ID.
Wi-Fi issues should be reported to the ICT department in the main building.
The computer labs are open during office hours; ask the lab technician for access.
Contact ICT support at the helpdesk for any technical or portal-related issues.

patterns: how do i find an internship
does the school help with attachment
when should i do my industrial attachment
how long is the attachment period
where can i do my industrial attachment
responses: The institute has an industrial liaison office that helps place students for attachment.
Attachment is usually done after your second year and lasts about three months.
Visit the industrial liaison office for a list of partner companies offering attachment.
You'll need an attachment letter from the school before reporting to your host company.
The liaison office can guide you on requirements and help match you with a host organisation.

patterns: how do i clear for graduation
do i need to clear fees before graduating
how do i get my certificate
responses: Graduation dates are announced by the academic office once results and clearance are finalised.
You must clear all fees and academic requirements before you can graduate.
Check the notice board or portal for the graduation clearance checklist.
Certificates are issued after successful clearance and the graduation ceremony.
Visit the academic office to confirm your clearance status ahead of graduation.

patterns: i want to file a complaint
i have an issue with a lecturer
how do i report a problem
i'm not happy with the service
who do i talk to about a complaint
i want to raise a concern
there is a problem i need to report
i need to escalate an issue
responses: You can file a formal complaint at the student affairs office or via the suggestion box.
Complaints can be submitted in writing to student affairs, who will follow up with you.
I'm sorry to hear that. Please raise it with the student affairs office so it can be addressed.
Feedback and complaints are welcome — the student affairs office handles these formally.
You can escalate serious concerns directly to the academic dean's office.
